input.background: Suppose you want to estimate \boldsymbol{\mu}_1-\boldsymbol{\mu}_2 with 95 \% confidence for a problem similar to the cholesterol illustrative example. You want your margin of error to be no greater than 0.25 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}.
input.question: We have established that s^2_{\text {pooled }}=0.24813, and will use this as a reasonable estimate of \sigma^2.

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: For 95% confidence, use z_{1-(0.05 / 2)}=1.96. The sample size required per group is n=\frac{2 \sigma^2 z^2}{m^2}=\frac{2 \cdot 0.24813 \cdot 1.96^2}{0.25^2}=30.5. Round this up to the next integer and resolve to use 31 individuals per group.

mu1_minus_mu2.description: The difference between the means of two populations, which we want to estimate with 95% confidence.

margin_of_error.description: The maximum allowable margin of error for the confidence interval estimation.

s2_pooled.description: The pooled sample variance, used as an estimate of the population variance sigma squared.

meta_info.caption: Sample size requirement for a confidence interval for $\\mu_1-\\mu_2$
